operatorName: Sniper7Kills LLC, stewards of the Gridmason project
transparencyLog: This instance anchors to the public Sigstore infrastructure (Fulcio for keyless certs, Rekor as the log) rather than operating its own CA/log (SPEC §2, GW-D17). Anyone can independently verify what this registry has shipped.
reviewPolicyIntro: The flagship's review policy is published in full here. It opens invite-only: only verified publishers may publish while the review roster is single-person (see the launch-phase disclosure in §4).

reviewTiers.covers: No executable content — layouts, page types, dashboards.
Plain-JS widget/plugin remotes (the common case): SDK-adherence static analysis + DOM-abuse heuristics over a signed source archive.

currentLatency: published on this page and refreshed as the roster reports; until automated latency reporting lands, the operator updates this figure manually
countersignCustodyNote: the countersign key is an offline key, held distinct from the reviewer's publishing identity, and its custody procedure is documented before launch — even while both the review and countersign roles are held by one person during the single-roster phase.
rosterNote: The flagship launches with a single-person review roster (GW-D18). Its size and the resulting waiver are disclosed above and flagged per affected release in the transparency log. The roster grows to ≥ 2 reviewers before open community publishing (Phase C).
revocationSeverityNote: actively-exploited or credential-path issues are killed immediately, with deployment notification within 24 hours; other revocations propagate within 1 hour online (next bundle sync for air-gapped hosts).
contactNote: Reach the Gridmason project maintainers through the gridmason GitHub organization. Publishing on the flagship is invite-only during the launch phase.
